member.name: Gill German
member.synopsis: Gill German is the Labour MP for Clwyd North , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Road Traffic Offences: Speed Limits
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment her Department has made of the effectiveness of the current system whereby proof of posting is treated as proof of delivery for notices of intended prosecution for speeding offences; and whether
writtenQuestions.answerText: Notices of Intended Prosecution are issued by the police, not by the Department for Transport. The Road Traffic Offenders Act 1988 requires, for certain offences including speeding, that a person is warned at the time of the offence, or that a summons or ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Working Hours
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, whether he has made an assessment of the adequacy of current statutory rest break entitlements under the Working Time Regulations 1998; and whether he plans to review these provisions to improve workin
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Working Time Regulations establish minimum standards for rest periods, including a 20-minute rest break after every 6 hours’ work, a minimum rest period of 11 hours each 24-hour period and a minimum rest period of 24 hours in any 7-day period. The gov...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.heading: Social Media: Children
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what steps her Department is taking to work with young people in developing policies on restrictions to social media use for under-16s.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government has engaged extensively with children and young people as part of the Growing up in the Online World consultation and National Conversation, including through a dedicated version of the consultation for young people, alongside youth‑led ses...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Social Media: Children

writtenQuestions.answerText: Protecting children online is a priority for the Secretary of State and this government. The Online Safety Act provides strong foundations for protecting children across the UK. We have always been clear that we will build on that foundation and published...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.answerText: Protecting children online is a priority for the Secretary of State and this government. The Online Safety Act provides strong foundations for protecting children across the UK. We have always been clear that we will build on that foundation and published...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: YouTube: Safety
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, for what reason YouTube has not been included in the Government’s online safety and age assurance pilot programmes; and whether YouTube is expected to be included in any future iteratio
writtenQuestions.answerText: The decision not to include YouTube as part of the Government’s child social media pilot study was due to the use of the platform by schools and youth organisations for teaching and other educational purposes. Next steps will be considered once the curren...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Natural Gas and Oil: Wales
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what contingency plans are in place to protect fuel supply, heating oil availability, fertiliser supplies and food distribution in Wales in the event of prolonged disruption to global oil and
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK benefits from a diverse and resilient fuel supply chain and remains well supplied across all fuel types. The Government works closely with industry to monitor supply, demand, and market conditions. Government’s long-standing contingency plans inclu...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Electricity: Prices
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, for what reason wholesale electricity prices are set by gas-fired generation under the marginal pricing system; and what assessment he has made of the impact of this pricing structure on hous
writtenQuestions.answerText: Our recent announcement on 21 April set out several measures that will help break the link between the price of gas and the price of electricity. These measures include voluntary long term fixed contracts and an updated Electricity Generators Levy. Margin...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Telephones: Fraud
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what assessment her Department has made of the scale of spoofing scams using UK telephone numbers; and whether she plans to strengthen obligations on network operators to prevent fraudu
writtenQuestions.answerText: The most recent Ofcom research on the scale of spoofing calls reveals that in February 2025, two in five phone users said they received a suspicious call in the last three months. Tackling fraud and pursuing the criminals behind it is a priority for the g...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Internet: Safety
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what assessment she has made of the effectiveness of online safety protections for vulnerable adults, including neurodivergent adults such as those with autism and ADHD; and whether she
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Online Safety Act places legal duties on platforms to make their services safer for all users, including vulnerable adults and those that are neurodivergent.Services are required to protect users from illegal content and activity online, which may imp...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.answerText: In response to the first question, I refer the Hon Member to the answers provided on 10 April in response to Question 123071, and on 28 January in response to Question 106997. In response to the second question, the UK and wider G7 countries have committe...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Energy: Prices
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of wholesale electricity prices being set by the marginal cost of gas generation on the extent to which consumer bills reflect lower-cost r
writtenQuestions.answerText: Marginal pricing incentivises the cheapest sources of energy production to provide as much power as possible, more expensive producers are only used when it is necessary to meet demand. The real problem is that we rely too much on volatile fossil fuels – ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Breast Cancer
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to improve UK-wide data collection on secondary breast cancer; and how such data is used to inform research, workforce planning, including specialist nurses, and assessments
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the importance of robust, comprehensive data on secondary, or metastatic, breast cancer to improve patient outcomes, inform research, and support effective workforce and service planning.Through the National Cancer Plan, for the ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: British Nationals Abroad: EU Countries
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, what discussions he has had with the EU on the 90/180-day rule for UK nationals visiting the Schengen area; and whether it plans to seek changes to that rule.
writtenQuestions.answerText: HMG officials and Ministers, including myself, regularly engage the EU and EU Member State counterparts on a range of issues affecting UK nationals. The UK and the EU allow for visa-free, short-term travel in line with their respective arrangements for th...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Cabinet Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Internet: Age Assurance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what assessment her Department has made of the likelihood of children and young people migrating to alternative online services if age verification is introduced unevenly across service
writtenQuestions.answerText: Ofcom will publish a report on the effectiveness of age assurance in terms of compliance with the duties under the Online Safety Act by July 2026, and a separate report on the role of app stores in protecting children by January 2027. We are also seeking ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Energy: Small Businesses
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what data his Department holds on the frequency and duration of estimated billing for small business energy customers.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Billing issues, for both domestic and non-domestic customers, are a matter for Ofgem as the sector regulator. Ofgem’s licence conditions require suppliers to take all reasonable steps to reflect accurate meter readings in bills or statements sent to custo...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Energy: Small Businesses
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of inaccurate or prolonged estimated energy billing on small and microbusinesses.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Billing issues, for both domestic and non-domestic customers, are a matter for Ofgem as the sector regulator. Ofgem’s licence conditions require suppliers to take all reasonable steps to reflect accurate meter readings in bills or statements sent to custo...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Absent Voting: British Nationals Abroad
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what guidance is issued to Returning Officers on the latest date by which postal ballot packs for overseas electors must be dispatched; and what monitoring is undertaken to ensur
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Electoral Commission provides guidance to Returning Officers including on the issuing of postal votes to overseas addresses. The Electoral Commission is also responsible for setting standards and monitoring the performance of Returning Officers.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Mobile Phones and Social Media: Children
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, if she will take steps to help ensure that official child right’s impact assessments are undertaken to inform the evaluation of different policy options during the consultation, Driving
writtenQuestions.answerText: We will assess a range of impacts when deciding how we will act on social media, including children’s rights and their wellbeing. To inform those assessments, we will consult children and young people directly through the national conversation and consult...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Care Homes: Visits

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Care Homes Regulations 2001/3965 were revoked by the Health and Social Care Act 2008 (Commencement No.16, Transitory and Transitional Provisions) Order 2010/807 on 1 October 2010. Regulation 26(2)(c) of the Care Homes Regulations 2001 is no longer in ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care
